xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: Electronic City, Hosur Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560 100, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Electronic City, Hosur Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560 100, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat: 11.The paid up capital figure has been rounded-off to the nearest crore. 18. Also applicable NIC Code 631: Data processing, hosting and related activities; web portals 19.a.Denotes the locations of our clients 19.b.Based on standalone financial statements under Ind AS as exports are considered in relation to India 20.a. 'Other than permanent’ employees includes contractors 20.a We do not have any workers. 20.b.Employees who have voluntarily disclosed their disabilities 21.Key Management Personnel are Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director (CEO & MD), Chief Financial Officer (CFO) and Company Secretary (CS). 23.Turnover rate : Voluntary attrition for IT services excluding business process management services, products and platforms.We do not have workers. 24.Our subsidiaries contribute data required for the preparation of this report. 25.Turnover & Net worth: As per the standalone financial statements under Ind AS 26.The Company does not track complaints from investors and shareholders separately. During the year, the Company has modified its policy of classifying shareholders grievances / complaints. 27. For the complete list of material topics, read ESG report 2024: Materiality and stakeholder engagement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfMainActivity: Software and IT consulting (GICS classification – Information Technology – Software and Services)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Software application development and maintenance, and IT consulting. Further details are provided in the Management Discussion and Analysis section of the Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Software application development and maintenance, IT consulting and digital services
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: Infosys Technologies S. de R. L. de C. V. (Infosys Mexico)
Infosys Public Services, Inc. USA (Infosys Public Services)
Infosys Financial Services GmbH (Formerly known as Panaya Gmbh)
Infosys Singapore Pte. Ltd. (formerly Infosys Consulting Pte. Ltd.)
Blue Acorn iCi Inc (formerly Beringer Commerce Inc)
Infosys Automotive and Mobility GmbH & Co. KG
Infosys Turkey Bilgi Teknolojileri Limited Sirketi
Infosys (Malaysia) SDN. BHD. (formerly Global Enterprise International (Malaysia) Sdn. Bhd.)
Wongdoody Gmbh (formerly known as oddity GmbH)
WongDoody (Shanghai) Co. Limited (formerly known as oddity (Shanghai) Co., Ltd.)
WongDoody limited (Taipei) (formerly known as oddity Limited (Taipei))
WongDoody d.o.o (formerly known as oddity code d.o.o)
Danske IT and Support Services India Private Limited (“Danske IT”)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: As on May 28, 2024, we have eight ASHI cases and five cases of discrimination at workplace, pending resolution.
All the pending cases in fiscal 2023 were resolved.
Complaints from customer projects primarily received through the complaints management system have been considered.
Complaints from customer projects primarily received through the complaints management system have been considered.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Climate change risks are increasingly manifesting in our business as strategic, physical and transitional (market and compliance) risks, which if not managed adequately, can affect our operations, reputation and profitability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Refer to the Infosys ESG data book 2023-24 for details on risk mitigations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Engaging clients on climate actions through our solutions
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: – Increased revenue through development and / or expansion of services to help our customers manage their climate change risks. – Savings from use of lower-emission sources of energy – Lead action globally on climate change through advocacy
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: We have metioned the identified opportunities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Inability to facilitate best-in-class employee experience may impact our ability to attract, hire, train, engage and retain talent
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: – Employee engagement and care – Holistic employee retention and recognition policies – Career and leadership development focus – Occupational health and safety measures
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Tech for Good platforms and solutions for e-governance, healthcare and education
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: – The development and adoption of advanced technologies, including smart automation and artificial intelligence, have the potential to raise productivity and solve larger challenges for the benefit of the community while facilitating the achievement of SDGs. Digital technologies and platforms have been used successfully in consumer technologies and provide an opportunity to apply these to ensure social good.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: We have metioned the identified opportunities
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: – Cyber attacks that breach our information network and / or failure to protect sensitive and confidential information of our stakeholders in accordance with applicable laws and contractual obligations may impact our operations, client satisfaction or result in significant regulatory penalties.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: – Robust cybersecurity strategy, framework, processes, policies and controls to improve cyber resilience. – Robust data privacy framework, policies, processes and controls – Multi-layered governance process with executive and the Board’s oversight to review cybersecurity and data privacy risks and our preparedness to mitigate and respond to such risks. – Continued investment in technologies to address the risks of the evolving cyber threat landscape – Close collaboration with cyber intelligence and forensic agencies to identify and prepare for emerging cybersecurity threats, periodic table-top exercises and keep the cybersecurity crisis plan up to date – Regular awareness programs and trainings along with a rigorous consequence management process – Periodic reviews, testing and audits
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Being recognized as industry leader in our information security practices and adoption of leading data privacy standards
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: – Increasing revenue from cybersecurity service offerings and solutions – Being recognized as an industry leader in our information security practices, and adoption of leading data privacy standards across global operations will result in higher client confidence.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: We have metioned the identified opportunities

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: In 2020, we became carbon neutral, 30 years ahead of the timeline set by the Paris Agreement. In October 2020, we launched our ESG vision and ambitions for 2030.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible:
“Infosys is committed to nurturing a sustainable and socially responsible business. The company’s ongoing ESG aspirations are reflected in the Infosys ESG Vision 2030 that articulates how the company can continue to be a well-governed organization and inclusive workplace for diverse talent with community strategies to leverage technology for good.”
Salil Parekh Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director
Information on ESG-related challenges, targets and achievements is available in the chapters Approaching value creation and Delivering value in the Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe:
1. Whistleblower Policy and Infosys Code of Conduct and Ethics (Policy)- General Counsel and Compliance officer (Implementation authority)- Audit Committee(Oversight authority)
2. Responsible Supply Chain and Supplier Diversity Policy and Supplier Code of Conduct (Policy)- Global head – Procurement (Implementation authority) - ESG Committee(Oversight authority)
3. CSR Policy(Policy)- Global Head – Corporate Accounting and Taxation (Implementation authority)- CSR Committee (Oversight authority)
4. ESG Vision 2030 (Policy)- Chief Financial Officer (Implementation authority) - ESG Committee(Oversight authority)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: The ESG Committee of the Board. Read more in the ESG Committee report in the Corporate governance report of this Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesManagementAndProcessDiscl: 1-c: Human Rights Statement and HSE policy are approved by executive leadership 1-c: CSR Policy is not extended to our suppliers
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: The Board members also completed an online ESG learning module covering global ESG disclosures, Infosys’ ESG performance and best practices. Refer to the Training of Board members section in the Corporate governance report in the Integrated Annual Report.
Infosys Code of Conduct and Ethics, climate change, environmental sustainability, social sustainability, data privacy and cybersecurity
Infosys Code of Conduct and Ethics, climate change, environmental sustainability, social sustainability, data privacy and cybersecurity
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Our Code of Conduct and Ethics complies with the legal requirements of applicable laws and regulations, including anti-bribery, anti-corruption and ethical handling of conflicts of interest. Additionally, we also have an Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption (ABAC) Policy (available in the Company intranet), which provides the requirements around ABAC in detail.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: The Company receives disclosure of interest at regular intervals from the Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator:1- Apart from year-long awareness campaigns through email on responsible business and related topics, all our employees have access to an exclusive learning channel on Lex, our internal learning platform and Infosys Springboard, our flagship digital learning platform.
Essential Indicator:2: Details of Penalties/Fines and all other disclosuers made under Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Obligations) Regulations, 2015 are available at https://www.infosys.com/investors/reports-filings/exchange-filings.html.
Essential Indicator:9- As per consolidated financial statements under Ind AS.
Leadership Indicator:1 5,600+ suppliers were enabled on ESG awareness programs through the ESG learning portal. Our assessment covers upstream value chain partners (suppliers).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Climate change and best practices in data capture, monitoring, and reporting on climate change disclosures, water management, waste,management, human rights, occupational health and safety, Anti-Bribery and Anti-Corruption
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImprovementsInEnviron: Education, training and assessment of employees to upskill and reskill. Technology spent to improve environmental and social producs and processes
Efficient equipment for cooling, lighting, renewable energy, water management, waste management and sustainable material
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableCondu: We are an IT services company, we don’t manufacture any products.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator:1- R& D and Capex data based on standalone financial statements under Ind AS.
Essential Indicator:2- All our procurements follow the principles of sustainable sourcing. We require suppliers to accept the supplier code of conduct, which is based on the UNGC Principles. We do not have a policy of computing the value of inputs sourced sustainably.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Infosys is committed to providing a safe and positive work environment as enshrined in our Code of Conduct. Employees and contract staff have access to a well-established robust grievance resolution mechanism known as resolution hubs where they can highlight matters or concerns faced at the workplace. For more information, refer to Resolution hubs available in the Management Discussion and Analysis section of the Integrated Annual Report.

Infosys is committed to providing a safe and positive work environment as enshrined in our Code of Conduct. Employees and contract staff have access to a well-established robust grievance resolution mechanism known as resolution hubs where they can highlight matters or concerns faced at the workplace. For more information, refer to Resolution hubs available in the Management Discussion and Analysis section of the Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Infosys recognizes and accords highest priority to safety and well-being of its employees and other relevant parties. Our HSE Policy enunciates our philosophy and commitment towards the management of key HSE aspects. In line with our strategy, our HSEMS at all Indian locations is certified to ISO 45001:2018 standard. At overseas locations, we have implemented processes based on legal requirements / internal benchmarks and have also included them in the internal audits cycle. We have established numerous interventions to address occupational health-related topics such as emotional well-being, mental health, ergonomics, safety, lifestyle diseases and more. Well-equipped occupational health centers are available at all our campuses in India. During the year, doctors and physiotherapists have helped employees and their families through virtual consultations leveraging our telemedicine portal. More details on Occupational Health and Safety are available on our website at https://www.infosys.com/about/corporate-responsibility/social/employee-wellbeing/occupational-health-safety.html.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: We proactively identify occupational health and safety risks, for all existing / new / modified activities, processes, products or services, and regulatory changes, including routine and non-routine activities. Risk assessment includes quarterly evaluation of incidents that have occurred. Hazardous conditions, if any, are identified and prioritized for elimination and control. Once the identified hierarchy of controls is implemented, the risk assessment is revisited to assess the residual risks. As Infosys is an IT / ITES company, there are no product risks, but there are those related to the provision of services like ergonomics at work and those associated with the operation of utilities and employees’ commute. Participation and consultation with relevant personnel involved in the activities is ensured during the risk assessments. Risks are also assessed before and post the development of new buildings. Experience from previous projects and current operations is also considered. We continually monitor our construction sites where infrastructure is being established. More details on Occupational Health and Safety are available on our website at https://www.infosys.com/about/corporate-responsibility/social/employee-wellbeing/occupational-health-safety.html.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE:
Infosys acknowledges that Occupational Health and Safety (OH&S) is one of the key aspects of sustainable business practices. OH&S policies, processes, and practices at Infosys promote physical, mental, and social well-being of employees in the workplace. The robust Health and Safety Management system at Infosys has earned us the certification of ISO 45001:2018 for all Indian locations, including Infosys Limited and its subsidiaries, in line with our HSE strategy.
The Company has the following measures to ensure a safe and healthy workplace: 1. Incident management: Incidents which include near misses / potential hazards / accidents are reported through internal applications, supervisors, or mails and root causes of incidents are identified, analyzed and appropriate corrective actions are taken to avoid recurrence or occurrence of incidents leading to injuries / losses. 2. OH&S Committee: Both employees and contract workers are a part of the Infosys OH&S Committee which helps in ensuring their active participation and consultation. 3. Training and awareness: Training includes awareness building, mock drills, classroom sessions and periodic demonstrations. HSEMS training is a part of our employee induction program. To enable continuous learning, a 4. HSE awareness module is available on Lex, our internal learning platform. Job-specific and generic trainings are conducted for contractual staff during induction and later through refresher trainings. 4. Safety interventions: We have always focused on building a culture of safety at Infosys. The safety systems in place include work permits, trainings, Lock Out Tag Out (LOTO), safety inspections, audits, operational controls, and monitoring. Policies have been established focusing on specific areas such as women’s safety, lone working, transport, travel, construction, amongst others. 5. Medical services: We have set up first aid centers on our campuses in India. Some of the first aid facilities are operational round the clock. Our telemedicine portal continues to operate and allows employees to consult the doctors at our occupational health centers in India online. Ambulance services are available at all our locations in India round the clock. 6. Health Risk Assessment: HRA is carried out annually based on inputs from the OHC. Being an IT / ITES company, the prevalent risks include ergonomics, Musculoskeletal Disorders (MSDs), emotional well-being, etc., associated with workplace, operation of utilities, and commute. Numerous initiatives, interventions, engagement virtual sessions, and process controls are in place to address these risks. 7. Programs on ergonomics: These include on-site physiotherapy centres, interventions by ergonomic experts and providing ergonomic infrastructure. 8. Physical and emotional well-being: At our locations in India, we have dietitians to provide counseling and guidance. We have also set up state-of-the-art gyms to train physical and mental fitness. 9. Programs for mothers: Pregna Care, a professional health care program is designed especially for expectant mothers and aims at providing maximum comfort to the mother. 10. Working environment: We also focus on improving the work environment by monitoring indoor air quality, lux and noise levels and promoting the use of green seal chemicals.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: There have been no significant risks / concerns arising from assessments of health and safety practices and working conditions. Stringent operation controls such as maker and checker control points are deployed across the operational areas. These are also monitored on a periodic basis.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple3ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator:1a. The field has been entered with "0" as the company does not directly provide well being measures to other than permanent employees. The company ensures that all mandated well-being measures for other than permanent employees is provided by the respective vendors and contractors as per the respective rules of the state. We provide onsite, proximity and network (near home) childcare support options for our employees in India, based on their preference. Essential Indicator 1.b The field has been entered "0" as we do not have any workers.
Essential Indicator:2 This table represents retirement benefits for the employees working in India. All our employees working outside India are eligible for retirement benefits according to applicable laws in the regions we operate. All eligible employees covered under the Employees State Insurance Act (“ESIC”), 1948 are provided the benefit. Pertains to contribution made by employers for employees who have opted for the same. Eligible employees are participants to superannuation retirement benefits. For FY-2024 and FY-2023 : We do not have workers.
Essential Indicator:5 100% of our permanent employees are eligible for parental leaves. The field for permanent workers has been entered as "0" as we do not have workers.
Essential Indicator:6 The field for workers and other than permanent workers has been entered as "No" as we do not have workers.
Essential Indicator:7 The field for worker is maked as "0" as we do not have workers.
Essential Indicator:8 Only for permanent employees and includes awareness programs. The field for traning for workers is mentioned as "0" as we do not have workers.
Essential Indicator 9: The company provides performance and career development reviews for all its permanent employees. During FY-24, out of 192671 male employees, eligible male employees were 157504 and out of 124569 female employees eligible female employees were 102420.Therefore 100% of the eligible employees have received performance and career development reviews.
Essential Indicator:11 India operations, Includes ‘other than permanent’ employees and Infosys does not have workers.
Essential Indicator:14 Our HSEMS is certified to ISO 45001:2018 standard audited by certifying bodies annually. The scope of HSEMS is applicable to all activities, which are a part of our operations and employees working for and on behalf of the Company, including deputees at client sites. Safety and well-being of our employees is accorded the highest priority. Our internal Corporate Certification Audits and Assessments Team (CCAT) conducts periodic assessments across Infosys locations annually. (Assessment for India Operations).
Leadership Indicator:1 The field for extending life insurance or compensatory package in the event of death for workers is marked as "No" as we do not have workers.
Leadership Indicator:3 The field for workers having suffered high consequence work-related injury / ill-health / fatalities has been marked as "0" as we do not have workers.
Leadership Indicator:5 We have completed ESG assessment of 328 top suppliers as at March 31, 2024.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksforComplaintsExplanator: All complaints filed during the year were resolved.
All complaints filed during the year were resolved.
All complaints filed during the year were resolved.
All complaints filed during the year were resolved.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: The Company periodically audits value chain partners to ensure timely deduction and deposit of statutory dues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: There were no significant risks / concerns arising from assessments of health and safety practices and working conditions of value chain partners.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: We identify and prioritize our stakeholders based on the impact of the Company on the stakeholders and the ability of the stakeholder groups to influence the functioning of the Company. As part of the materiality assessment, we have identified six key stakeholder groups: Investors/shareholders, clients, employees and sub-contractors, suppliers/partners, government/regulators and the community. The ESG Committee of the Board also approves the continued relevance of the material matters encompassing the views of our stakeholders on an annual basis.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: We have identified our most material issues through a data-driven and consultative exercise. The material topics were shortlisted and prioritized based on their impact on our stakeholders and our business. Periodic meetings were held to update the sub-committees of the Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Our material topics were shortlisted and prioritized based on their impact on our stakeholders and our business. The material topics and the linked ambitions can be accessed through our ESG vision 2030 and performance updates through our annual ESG Reports.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: Infosys Foundation was set up to support underprivileged sections of society, create opportunities and strive towards a more equitable society. Infosys contributes as a part of its CSR initiatives to Infosys Foundation. The Foundation engages with the community, especially vulnerable and marginalized stakeholder groups, in a variety of focus areas. For information on the percentage of beneficiaries of the CSR projects, refer to Principle 8, Q.6 (Leadership Indicators) in this report. Read the Infosys Foundation annual reports at https://www.infosys.com/infosys-foundation/.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Investors calls, emails, and personal meetings, Analyst meets, Conferences (including broker-led events) , Quarterly results, Annual General Meeting, Sustainability report, Financial reports, India stock exchange filings (NSE and BSE), US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) filings, Press releases, Social media
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1. To answer queries of investors on Infosys’ ambitions and progress, 2. Build transparency with existing and potential investors
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Employee satisfaction surveys, Employee resource groups, Resolution hubs and whistleblower mechanism, Communication blogs, Development centers’ engagement initiatives, InfyMe (internal intranet) and podcasts
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1.Communicate the employee value proposition 2.Keep a finger on pulse of employee engagement 3.Provide an opportunity to raise concerns Training and development 4.Employee recognition and engagement activities 5.Performance review and career development 6. Employee health, safety, and well-being
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Client visits and meetings Customer satisfaction surveys Annual customer conclaves
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1.Engage with clients on Infosys solutions and services, including climate change solutions 2.Seek client feedback on our solutions and services and continuously improve to meet their expectations 3.Develop relationships and partnerships with clients enabling delivery of high-quality client services and solutions 4.Communicate Infosys’ credentials including ESG credentials
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Engagement with government and global forums Policy advocacy and representations Engagement with industry bodies which have government participation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1.Share ESG best practices 2.Participate in forums to strengthen the adoption of responsible business practices 3.Participation / aid in drafting regulations / public policies 4.Share a perspective on global standards and alignment with international benchmarking
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Meetings with associations / NGOs Local community interactions Social media
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: 1.Enable access to digital skilling 2.Serve the community through Tech for Good programs in education, healthcare, and governance 3.Enable participation of diverse communities in the economy 4.CSR engagement through Infosys Foundation
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Suppliers meet, Sambandh (bi-annual) ESG report Supplier engagement on ESG
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: Engage with suppliers to strengthen awareness through training
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Infosys is committed to providing a safe and positive work environment as enshrined in our Code of Conduct. Employees and contract staff have access to a well-established robust grievance resolution mechanism known as resolution hubs where they can highlight matters or concerns faced at the workplace including those pertaining to human rights. For more information, refer to Resolution hubs available in the Management Discussion and Analysis section of the Integrated Annual Report.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksforComplaintsExplanator:
Incidents of sexual harassment were reviewed as per the requirements of POSH Act in India and as per the established grievance redressal process. As on May 28, 2024, we have eight ASHI cases and five cases of discrimination at workplace, pending resolution.
A robust feedback mechanism ensures employee feedback and concerns are heard and addressed in a timely manner. Read more at https://www.infosys.com/about/esg/social/employee-wellbeing/resolution-hubs.html

Incidents pertaining to discrimination were reviewed as per the established grievance redressal process for HEAR. As on May 28, 2024, we have eight ASHI cases and five cases of discrimination at workplace, pending resolution. A robust feedback mechanism ensures employee feedback and concerns are heard and addressed in a timely manner. Read more at https://www.infosys.com/about/esg/social/employee-wellbeing/resolution-hubs.html

Incidents of sexual harassment were reviewed as per the requirements of POSH Act in India and as per the established grievance redressal process.All the pending cases in fiscal 2023 were resolved. A robust feedback mechanism ensures employee feedback and concerns are heard and addressed in a timely manner. Read more at https://www.infosys.com/about/esg/social/employee-wellbeing/resolution-hubs.html

Incidents pertaining to discrimination were reviewed as per the established grievance redressal process for HEAR.All the pending cases in fiscal 2023 were resolved. A robust feedback mechanism ensures employee feedback and concerns are heard and addressed in a timely manner. Read more at https://www.infosys.com/about/esg/social/employee-wellbeing/resolution-hubs.html
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: Infosys’ non-retaliation policy is an embodiment of our values and a cornerstone of our Code. Infosys commits to protect the complainant and ensures that they are not retaliated against because of any report that they raise in good faith. Infosys does not tolerate any form of retaliation (whether by a manager, co-worker or otherwise) against an individual because he or she made a good faith report of an integrity concern. This protection also extends to anyone who assists with or cooperates in an investigation or reports of an integrity concern or question. We support those who support our values.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: There were no significant risks / concerns arising from the human rights assessments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: Infosys is committed to providing a healthy and safe work environment, which is integral to our Code of Conduct. Training on Infosys values and the Code of Conduct and Ethics, in which our stand on human rights is enshrined, is an integral part of the induction program for new employees. Every employee at Infosys is mandated to take the Smart Awareness Quiz (SAQ) every year, which contains learning and assessments on the Code and human rights-related topics. Year-round email campaigns on human rights topics remind employees of the expectations of maintaining a respectful workplace for everyone. A periodic ‘Pulse’ survey rolled out to employees solicits feedback on various topics, including human rights. All suppliers must mandatorily sign the Infosys Supplier Code of Conduct, which contains Human Rights clauses. Our ESG learning portal for our suppliers includes topics on human rights, and our supplier ESG assessments include human rights-related topics.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: There were no significant risks / concerns arising from the assessments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple5ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator 1: The Code of Conduct expresses Infosys’ commitment to conducting business ethically and in accordance with the values of the Company while respecting the human rights of all stakeholders. All employees are trained on the Code during their induction into the organization. The suppliers are covered through the Supplier Code of Conduct and in-person sessions. Our Human Rights Statement is a part of the Code of Conduct which is approved by the Board on an annual basis.
Essential Indicator 2:This table is for India only. The number of male employees in India is: 161214 and Female employees is: 104118 and therefore for equal to minimum wage, percentage employees covered are Male:0.83 and Female:1.67. The number of male employees in India is: 161214 and Female employees is: 104118 and therefore for more than minimum wage, percentage employees covered are Male:99.17 and Female:98.33.
For other than permanent employees the field has been marked as "0" as the vendors and contractors are required to adhere to statutory compliances as per the respective rules of the state.
Essential Indicator 3 a: Data as on March 31, 2024, Remuneration to Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director (CEO & MD) has been included in KMP. Includes permanent employees only All median remuneration values are in INR Lakhs.
Essential Indicator 3 b:Includes permanent employees only
Essential Indicator 10: Assessment of the year for India operations, as per the assessment plan for the year
Leadership Indicator 4: We have completed ESG assessment of 328 top suppliers as at March 31, 2024.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple6ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator 1: Total energy consumed (A + B + C + D + E + F) includes global energy consumption The revenue from operations has been adjusted for PPP based on the latest PPP conversion factor published by the IMF- for India. For the years ended March 31, 2024 and March 31, 2023, it is 22.401 and 22.167, respectively.
Essential Indicator 3:Total volume of water withdrawal (i + ii + iii + iv + v) includes global water consumption.
Essential Indicator 4: Water sent to third parties with treatment in centralized sewage treatment plants, is managed by local authorities. Total water discharged includes one leased location in India and all leased overseas locations. Water is discharged to municipal sewers and finally treated.
Essential Indicator 7: Scope 1 emissions cover all owned offices (India, US and China) and leased offices in India; Leased space in overseas locations will not be considered as it is falls in de-minimus for diesel / natural gas consumption. Scope 2 includes India and overseas locations.
Essential Indicator 9: Other hazardous waste includes used oil and waste residue containing oil, toner, and cartridges, discarded containers, and other hazardous miscellaneous waste. Other non-hazardous waste generated includes food, garden waste, metal waste, paper waste, thermocol, textile, glass waste and other inert waste. Total (A + B + C + D + E + F + G + H) includes waste generated in India, overseas owned (Indianapolis and Shanghai) and e-waste for all locations globally. Waste intensity in terms of physical output (MT / capita / annum) excludes C&D waste.
Leadership Indicator 1: Includes one leased location in India, and overseas leased offices in water stress regions. Water sent to third parties with treatment in centralized sewage treatment plants, is managed by local authorities.
Leadership Indicator 2: Starting this year, emissions associated with employees’ hotel stays are calculated and included in the business travel emissions. For Fiscal year 2023- addition of emissions associated with hotel accomodation in business travel. Refer ESG Databook Annexure 4:GHG Emissions for details
Leadership Indicator 7 : We have completed ESG assessment of 328 top suppliers as at March 31, 2024.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: All sewage generated on campus in our India locations is treated in the in-house sewage treatment plants, and the recycled water is used for irrigation, HVAC, and flushing purposes. In some of our smaller leased offices with limited space or lesser operational control, the wastewater is discharged into municipal sewers, which undergo further treatment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: Infosys has been carbon neutral since fiscal 2020 across Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions. Our approach to carbon neutrality is based on reducing and avoiding emissions through energy efficiency and renewable energy. Energy efficiency is achieved through super-efficient new buildings, industry-leading Energy Performance Index (EPI) <70 kWh/m2-yr, real-time monitoring and optimization of building operations through smart building systems, and retrofits in existing buildings. Infosys has implemented several retrofit projects (across air conditioning, lighting, and UPS systems) in the past and achieved a connected electrical load reduction of 36 MW across campuses in India. In fiscal 2024, we have undertaken a retrofit project to replace direct FCU units with chilled water units of air conditioning, which saved 1.1 lakh kWh and reduced emissions by 80.84 tCO2e. To achieve our goal of transitioning to clean energy, we have installed 60.2 MWp of solar PV capacity.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: The 5R principle –’ Reduce, Reuse, Refurbish, Repurpose and Recycle’ – forms the foundation of Infosys’ waste management strategy. Through our efforts, we turn waste into resources and thus support a circular economy. Infosys has adopted a comprehensive waste management policy that emphasizes the collection, sorting and disposal of waste. Waste management practices at Infosys include source segregation, secondary segregation, effective waste processing and collection, recycling and efficient disposal of all waste in accordance with relevant laws. This helps reduce negative impacts on the environment. We seek to uphold our ambition of ‘zero waste to landfill’ through active minimization combined with technology investment in recycling and streamlining systems and processes. This year, we achieved TRUE Zero Waste Certification for our owned campuses in Bengaluru, Chennai Mcity and Pune Phase-2 through Green Business Certification Inc. (GBCI). The e-waste is always sent back to original manufacturers (under buyback schemes) or authorized recyclers who provide us certificates on the successful recycling and recovery of the material. Other hazardous waste like biomedical waste, oil-soaked cotton, oil filters from DG sets, and other trash are designated by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) and the State Pollution Control Board (SPCB). These agencies incinerate the waste in compliance with rules and regulations. The resulting ash is diverted for use in the production of cement in a few places and delivered to Treatment, Storage, Disposal Facility (TSDF) landfills for safe disposal.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameAndBriefDetailsOfProject: Amendment in EC of IT Park at MIDC, Rajiv Gandhi, Infotech Park-II, Hinjewadi, Pune, Maharashtra by M/s Infosys Ltd.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: Infosys has a Business Continuity Management System (BCMS) called Phoenix, certified by ISO 22301:2019 Security and Resilience – Business Continuity Management Standard. This program is designed to ensure seamless continuity of business and the utmost safety of employees and organization assets while continuously meeting client expectations. The BCMS program provides a robust framework for planning, establishing, implementing, operating, monitoring, reviewing, maintaining, and continually improving business continuity measures across Infosys and its subsidiaries as per the global BCMS strategy. Infosys has a business continuity and disaster recovery plan called the Phoenix plan at the corporate level, and comprehensive business continuity plans are created at three operational levels covering business functions, locations, and accounts. Integrated into our Enterprise Risk Management Framework, the BCMS plans guide our typical response to events, such as catastrophes and natural or human-made disasters, which could disrupt or severely constrain our operations. This covers various crisis scenarios as part of detailed risk assessments for functions, locations, and accounts, which are documented with mitigation plans along with controls put in place. The management system has been continuously validated across levels through tests and exercises, and various incidents have been successfully tackled without any significant business continuity or employee safety impacts. An efficient business continuity management policy has enabled us to maintain the status quo during disasters.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: As per ESG assessment of 328 top suppliers, no significant adverse impacts to environment were identified.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Received an Indian patent (No. 473792) for the product Retroshade
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Retroshade is a shading device that will help conserve energy and improve thermal comfort.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: ‘Retroshade’ can eliminate direct solar radiation on the glass façade, which will help reduce the solar heat gains on the building envelope.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: We continue to expand our green building portfolio with the highest level of green certification.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: We have achieved highest green building rating for 29.6 million square feet which includes owned and leased premises.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InitiativeUndertaken: Innovative technologies in our new buildings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForDetailsOfInitiativeT: Our new buildings are equipped with innovative technologies like shading devices, radiant cooling and high performance façade systems such as double-glazed systems with Argon-filled gas. Also, all our new buildings are undergoing LCA to assess the embodied carbon footprint, among the first in the industry.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:OutcomeOfTheInitiative: The innovative technologies used in our new buildings have resulted in Energy performance index (EPI) of lower than 70 kWh/m2/year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheArea: There are 10 water stress zones : India, Australia, Israel, Mauritius, Mexico, Philippines, Romania, South Africa, Spain, UAE
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple7ExplanatoryText: Essential Indicator 1: The Company has affiliations with various trade and industry chambers associations and these are tracked and managed by units globally.The list in 1:b is indicative.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: National Association of Software and Services Companies (NASSCOM)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Meetings and submissions to the EU Commission, Council and Parliament
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: Artificial Intelligence adoption within UK DWP
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: Meetings with the ministry accompanied by written briefings
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PublicPolicyAdvocated: Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill, India
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MethodResortedForSuchAdvocacy: The Company actively participated in drafting the India DPDP Bill
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: We track complaints, if any, from beneficiaries of our CSR projects. Complaints are received at feedback_if@infosys.com. In fiscal 2024, no complaints and grievances were received.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText:
Essential Indicator 1: SIA act applicable only in India
Essential Indicator 2: SIA act applicable only in India
Essential Indicator 4: Values for Fiscal year 2023 changed because of change in computational methodology
Essential Indicator 5: Permanent employees at our India locations have been considered.
Leadership Indicator 2: 109 out of the 112 aspirational districts were covered in fiscal 2024.
Leadership Indicator 6: Women, children and people with differently-abled are the main vulnerable groups identified. Beneficiary count is arrived based on the progress reports and emails received from beneficiaries.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject: Society for Advancement of Human Endeavor
Improved cook stove projects – Udaipur Urja Initiatives
Improved cookstove projects – Envirofit India Private Ltd
Sri Jayadeva Institute of Cardiovascular Sciences & Research
Improved cook stove projects – Global Himalayan Expedition
Chennai flood relief efforts – Sri Ramakrishna Sevashrama
Punjab and Himachal Pradesh flood relief efforts
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StateOfCSRProjectsUndertaken: Various districts – with spend less than one lakh
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AspirationalDistrictOfCSRProje: Various districts – with spend less than one lakh
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: We do not track this separately.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: We prioritize our clients’ satisfaction and have a comprehensive system in place to address their feedback and complaints. Our clientele, which spans diverse industry verticals, is equipped with multiple channels to voice their concerns and provide feedback. Every complaint is treated with the appropriate level of attention. Our dedicated teams meticulously analyze the issues raised, devise suitable solutions and implement them effectively. We place great emphasis on transparency towards our clients throughout the process. Therefore, we keep them informed of progress and seek their approval for corrective actions. In addition to addressing complaints, we proactively engage with our clients to understand their expectations, gather feedback and gain insight into their future outlook. This valuable information is crucial to our strategic planning and continuous improvement initiatives. Doing so ensures that our services align with our clients’ evolving needs and expectations fostering a strong and enduring business relationship.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarkForConsumerComplaints: We are a B2B company. The promotions we do is with regard to our services and thought leadership. The provision of services is governed by contracts between the parties.
Material incident and includes a substantiated data privacy complaint.
Complaints from customer projects that are raised through the complaints management system have been considered.
We are a B2B company. The promotions we do is with regard to our services and thought leadership. The provision of services is governed by contracts between the parties.
Complaints from customer projects that are raised through the complaints management system have been considered.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur: Yes. Infosys has a holistic and comprehensive cybersecurity framework – SEED- aligned to NIST’s CyberSecurity Framework (CSF) and supported by supplementary policies, processes, procedures, and standards to achieve and sustain enterprise-level information security objectives. Read more at https://www.infosys.com/about/corporateresponsibility/governance/information-management.html. Infosys has a Data Privacy Policy published on the Company’s intranet, which demonstrates the Management’s commitment to data privacy across all Infosys operations, including those involving service providers. To ensure complete transparency, we provide privacy notices at the data collection point for internal and external data subjects. The privacy statement for external data subjects is also readily available on the Infosys website at, Personal Information Privacy Statement | Infosys.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: Infosys has reviewed its cybersecurity posture, technology, and threat landscape to build further fortification and strengthen its cyber defense capabilities. As an organization, we continue to review and strengthen our cybersecurity processes and controls across our entire network in line with industry best practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfImpactOfDataBreachesE: Impact of data breach for the incident reported is a part of Infosys’ public disclosure. Incident management is ongoing.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TheEntityHasNotApplicableProdu: We carry out surveys to gauge customer satisfaction for our major services. Customer-focused excellence demands constant sensitivity to changing and emerging customer requirements and close attention to the voice of the customer. We interact with our clients regularly across multiple platforms. In addition to various client interactions, we have adopted a formal and robust approach in the form of an annual Client Value Survey. The survey enables us to understand the client’s expectations and needs comprehensively and serves as one of the inputs for us when making investment decisions. The survey framework includes a structured questionnaire, and the feedback is collected through a web survey hosted by an independent organization.
